The residual structural risks to adjacent historical buildings are classic residual risks that must be transferred to the business for ongoing monitoring, as they persist beyond the project's lifespan. All three items are appropriate content for follow-on action recommendations. B is incorrect: While the concessions were formally accepted, they still represent deviations from specification that the operational team needs to know about, as they may require remedial action in the future. Similarly, an unaddressed change request may represent a genuine business need that the city council should consider pursuing post-project. Excluding these items would leave the city council without important information about the as-built state of the shopping centre and unaddressed opportunities. C is incorrect: The fact that an item has been formally decided during the project does not preclude it from appearing in follow-on action recommendations. An accepted concession or a deferred change request may have operational consequences that the business needs to manage. The purpose of follow-on action recommendations is to inform the operational business, not to re-litigate project decisions. D is incorrect: The Project Manager is responsible for preparing follow-on action recommendations, in consultation with the project management team. This is explicitly stated in PRINCE2 7 as a recommended action for the Project Manager during closing a project. The CEO of BuildyBrick (Senior Supplier) may be consulted, but accountability for preparing this management product lies with the Project Manager, not the contractor.

topic: Closing a Project – Premature vs Planned Closure and Management Products
question: Halfway through Stage 3 of the LouisShopping project, a major archaeological discovery is made during the underground parking excavations. The Portfolio Director has decided that the project must be closed prematurely as the discovery means the original shopping centre design is no longer viable and a completely new project brief will be needed. The Project Manager must now carry out the prepare premature closure activity. Which of the following actions is MOST appropriate for the Project Manager to take FIRST?

options.A: Immediately disband the project management team and off-board all team members, as the project is closing and there is no further need for the project management structure once premature closure has been confirmed by the Portfolio Director.
options.B: Prepare an exception report for the Project Board explaining that stage tolerances have been breached due to the archaeological discovery, and await the Project Board's decision before commencing any closure activities.
options.C: Review and update the business case to reflect the impact of the archaeological discovery, document any uncompleted work, outstanding issues, and residual risks, and prepare follow-on action recommendations so that the business is fully informed of the situation at closure.
options.D: Archive all project data immediately in line with the digital and data management approach and close all project registers, as preserving data integrity is the highest priority when a project is closed prematurely.

rationale: C is correct: During premature closure, the Project Manager must still carry out the key activities of closing a project, adapted to the premature context. This includes reviewing and updating the business case to reflect actual costs and the changed situation, documenting uncompleted work (the partially completed construction), outstanding issues (the archaeological discovery and its implications), and residual risks (structural implications, site security, etc.), and preparing follow-on action recommendations. These actions ensure the business has a complete and accurate picture of the project's status at the point of closure and can make informed decisions about the next steps, including commissioning a new project. A is incorrect: While disbanding the project management team is an eventual outcome of project closure (covered under the Organizing practice in Table 19.3), it should not be the first action. The Project Manager must first ensure all management products are in order, follow-on actions are documented, and the project board has approved closure. Premature disbandment without completing closure activities would leave the city council without essential information and could create governance gaps. B is incorrect: An exception report is used when stage or project level tolerances are forecast to be exceeded during the delivery of a stage, allowing the Project Board to decide on the way forward. In this scenario, the Portfolio Director has already made the decision to close the project prematurely, so the context has moved beyond the need for an exception report. The Project Manager should now focus on executing the prepare premature closure activity, not re-triggering the exception escalation process. D is incorrect: While archiving project data and closing registers are indeed activities that occur during closing a project (as specified in Table 19.3 under the Progress and other practices), they are not the first priority. The Project Manager must first ensure the business case is updated, follow-on actions are captured, and the project board is briefed. Archiving and closing registers are important but come after the substantive closure activities have been completed, not before.
